prompt:
Cellar ledger tool for the wine bar's stockroom. A refactor last week split the old single header into rack.h/bin.h and ever since, this branch will not build — and the errors CONTRADICT each other between files. From `make -k test`:

    g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c cellar.cpp
    rack.h:12:17: error: 'Bin' was not declared in this scope
    rack.h:12:20: error: template argument 1 is invalid
    bin.h:15:20: error: redefinition of 'std::string fmt_qty(int)'
    g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c report.cpp
    bin.h:11:11: error: 'Rack' does not name a type
    rack.h:16:20: error: redefinition of 'std::string fmt_qty(int)'
    g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_cellar.cpp
    rack.h:12:17: error: 'Bin' was not declared in this scope
    bin.h:15:20: error: redefinition of 'std::string fmt_qty(int)'
    test_cellar.cpp:31:32: error: too many initializers for 'Rack'
    make: Target 'test' not remade because of errors.

So compiling cellar.cpp, the compiler says Bin doesn't exist and points at rack.h. Compiling report.cpp, it says RACK doesn't exist and points at bin.h. Same two headers, opposite complaints, and each header plainly defines the thing the other one is missing. Meanwhile fmt_qty is 'redefined' — and to be fair, I can see a fmt_qty in each header, and they don't even agree with each other, which probably explains why some old labels came out looking like 'x12' when the ledger and the tests have always wanted '12 btl'.

Heads-up from the hour I spent flailing: this one is layered. When I hacked at the header errors I got a brand-new complaint out of bin.h that wasn't there before, and after I bandaged THAT, the LINKER piled on about some function being defined multiple times in cellar.o and report.o. I reverted everything; it's all yours in its original broken state.

The rendering behavior is settled — ledger rows, padding, the btl units, rack back-references, stocktake totals — all pinned by the tests, which pass on main. Please make `make test` build and pass without touching test_cellar.cpp, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
notes: 5C4 item 6: include-order and ODR. g++ 16.1.1 / ld.bfd, -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra -Werror. Four planted defects: (1) circular includes — rack.h includes bin.h (needs full Bin for vector<Bin>) while bin.h includes rack.h back for a mere back-pointer; the include guards make the SECOND header in any TU see an undeclared type, so bin-first TUs (cellar.cpp, test) blame rack.h ('Bin was not declared') while the rack-first TU (report.cpp) blames bin.h ('Rack does not name a type') — the deliberately contradictory dump is the lesson; fix: bin.h forward-declares `struct Rack;` and drops the include. (2) The catalog's inline-defined-differently ODR item, realized as a compile-visible redefinition so it cannot be IFNDR (probed: one inline def per TU links silently even when bodies differ, so the seed puts both variants in headers that meet in every TU): fmt_qty duplicated during the split, rack.h's stale 'x12' variant vs bin.h's '12 btl'; the tests call fmt_qty directly and pin '1 btl'/'0 btl', so keeping the wrong copy fails behaviorally. (3) CHAIN-REVEAL (documented per 5C law, flagged in prompt as 'a brand-new complaint out of bin.h'): bin.h uses std::string but only got <string> transitively through the circular rack.h include — removing that include reveals it; fix adds <string> to bin.h. (4) CHAIN-REVEAL #2 (link stage, prompt-flagged): cellar.cpp and report.cpp each define a file-local format_row with the same signature but different bodies — 'multiple definition' at link once compiles succeed; fix wraps each in an anonymous namespace, and merging them instead cannot satisfy both pinned layouts ('pinot 19   | 12 btl' vs 'North Wall: 2 bins, 17 btl'). Reference fix: bin.h forward-decl + <string>, delete rack.h's fmt_qty, two anonymous namespaces.
